summary:
The “needle” was a memory-ordering bug in the single-producer / single-consumer ring buffer.

Fix applied in ring_buffer.c:

1. Producer (ringbuf_push)
   • load read_idx with memory_order_acquire  
   • store updated write_idx with memory_order_release

2. Consumer (ringbuf_pop)
   • load write_idx with memory_order_acquire  
   • store updated read_idx with memory_order_release

These release/acquire pairs guarantee the producer’s data write becomes visible to the consumer before the index is observed, and vice-versa, on all architectures.

Running ./test.sh now builds, runs the stress tests (fall-back multi-run path when TSan is unavailable) and reports:

PASS: All tests passed — no races, correct output
